content: If 6 letter words, with or without meaning can be formed out of these letters of the word "MATHEMATICS", repetition of letters is not allowed is $960 P$. Then, ' $P$ ' equals
explanation:
Unique letters in the word "MATHEMATICS" is

$$=8(M, A, T, H, E, I, C, S)$$

Total ways of selecting 6 letters out of 8 unique letters $={ }^8 C_6=28$

and total arrangements of 6 selected letters

$$=6!=720$$

Therefore, total numbers of 6 letters word

$$=720 \times 28=20160$$

According to the question,

$$960 P=20160 \Rightarrow P=\frac{20160}{960}=21$$

content: 6 couples decided to form a committee of four members, the number of different committees that can be formed in which no couple finds place is
explanation:
6 couples are as follows:

$$H_1 W_1, H_2 W_2, H_3 W_3, H_4 W_4, H_5 W_5, H_6 W_6$$

Number of ways of selecting four couples out of

$$6={ }^6 C_4$$

If no couple can finds a place then only one member of each group is selected.

So, Required number of committees

$$\begin{aligned} & ={ }^6 C_4 \times{ }^2 C_1 \times{ }^2 C_1 \times{ }^2 C_1 \times{ }^2 C_1 \\ & =\frac{6.5}{2} \times 16=240 \end{aligned}$$

content: Out of 9 consonants and 4 vowels, how many words of 4 consonants and 3 vowels can be formed?
explanation:
Number of ways of selecting (4 consonants and 3 vowels )

$$={ }^9 C_4 \times{ }^4 C_3=\frac{9 \times 8 \times 7 \times 6}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} \times 4=504$$

$$\because$$ Each group contains 7 letters

$$\therefore$$ Number of ways of arranging 7 letters among themselves $$=7 !=5040$$

Hence, required number of ways

$$=504 \times 5040=2540160$$

content: The sum of all the numbers of four different digits that can be made using the digits 0, 1, 2 and 3 is
explanation:
Required sum $$=$$ (sum of all the numbers formed by the digits $$0,1,2,3$$) $$-$$ (sum of all the numbers, having 0 at thousand place)

$$\begin{aligned} & =(0+1+2+3) \times(4-1) !\left(\frac{10^4-1}{10-1}\right) \\ & -(1+2+3)(3-1) !\left(\frac{10^3-1}{10-1}\right) \\ & =6 \times 3 !\left(\frac{10^4-1}{9}\right)-6 \times 2 !\left(\frac{10^3-1}{9}\right) \\ & =4\left(10^4-1\right)-\frac{4}{3} \times 999 \\ & =40000-4-1332=38664 \end{aligned}$$

content: There are 50 intermediate stations on a railway line from one terminus to another. The number of ways a train can stop at 3 of these intermediate stations if no two of these stopping stations are to be consecutive, are
explanation:
Let $$x, y, z$$ and $$t$$ be the numbers of stations before first stoppage, between first and second stoppage, between second and third stoppage, after third stoppage respectively, then $$x \geq 0, y \geq 1, z \geq 1, t \geq 0$$, and $$x+y+z+t=50-3$$

Let $$y=1+y_1$$ and $$z=1+z_1$$, so that $$y_1 \geq 0, z_1 \geq 0$$ and $$x+y_1+z_1+t=50-5$$.

The number of non-negative integral solution of

$$\begin{aligned} x+y_1+z_1+t & =50-5 \text { is }={ }^{50-5+4-1} C_{4-1} \\ = & { }^{48} C_3=\frac{48 \times 47 \times 46}{6}=17296 \end{aligned}$$

content: An urn contains 5 red marbles, 4 black marbles and 3 white marbles. Then the number of ways in which 4 marbles can be drawn so that at the most three of them are red is
explanation:
It is given that an urn contains 5 red marbles, 6 black marbles and 3 white marbles. Then the number of ways in which 4 marbles can be drawn so that at the most three of them are red is = (Total number of ways to drawn 4 marble) $$-$$ (Number of ways to drawn 4 marbles so that all are red)

$$\begin{aligned} & ={ }^{12} C_4-{ }^5 C_4=\frac{12 \times 11 \times 10 \times 9}{4 \times 3 \times 2}-5 \\ & =(11 \times 5 \times 9)-5=5(99-1)=5 \times 98 \\ & =490 \end{aligned}$$
